variantCoverage.closing: Not sure which variant your Land Rover Discovery is? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact sub‑model, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly.

engineGuide.h2: Land Rover Discovery Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Land Rover Discovery across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Defined Discovery 4 refinement, but timing chain wear and crankshaft fatigue drive frequent replacement requests.
compatibleVariants: Discovery 4 TDV6 S, Discovery 4 TDV6 SE, Discovery 4 TDV6 XS, Discovery 4 SDV6 SE, Discovery 4 SDV6 HSE, Discovery 4 SDV6 Autobiography, Discovery 4 SDV6 Landmark, Discovery 4 SDV6 Metropolitan Edition, Discovery 4 SDV6 Commercial
commonFailures: Crankshaft main bearing failure causing catastrophic bottom-end damage (often 60k-80k miles)
Timing chain tensioner wear leading to chain stretch and top-end misalignment (typically 90k+ miles)
EGR cooler and oil cooler leaks contaminating intake charge and reducing compression
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ30D Land Rover Discovery engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Replaced older V8 units, bringing PSA-Ford joint-venture efficiency and refined highway torque to the Discovery 3.
compatibleVariants: Discovery 3 TDV6 SE, Discovery 3 TDV6 HSE, Discovery 3 TDV6 Commercial
commonFailures: Injector copper seal failure causing cylinder pressurisation and head damage (commonly 70k-100k miles)
Turbocharger actuator arm play and bearing wear under sustained load
Coolant crossover pipe cracking leading to rapid overheating and head warping
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ27D Land Rover Discovery engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2 Litre Diesel
entries.history: A PSA-Ford collaboration bringing emissions compliance and commercial durability to mid-generation Discovery models.
compatibleVariants: Discovery 4 TD4 SE, Discovery 4 SD4 HSE, Discovery 4 SD4 Commercial, Discovery 4 TD4 S
commonFailures: Oil pump drive gear wear causing sudden low oil pressure and bearing spin (typically 50k-70k miles)
DPF differential pressure sensor failures triggering limp mode and excessive soot retention
Injector coding drift leading to rough idle and fuel dilution in sump
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ200D Land Rover Discovery engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Discovery 5’s efficient Ingenium four-cylinder, balancing urban refinement with notable EGR and turbo carbon sensitivity.
compatibleVariants: Discovery 5 TD4 S, Discovery 5 D165 SE, Discovery 5 D200 R-Dynamic, Discovery 5 D250 HSE, Discovery 5 TD4 Commercial
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump cam follower wear causing metal contamination in fuel rails (often 60k+ miles)
Variable geometry turbo actuator sticking due to carbon buildup on vanes
EGR valve matrix failure leading to excessive intake restriction and reduced MPG
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ20D Land Rover Discovery engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Land Rover’s first electronic Discovery diesel, delivering strong towing capacity but plagued by injector wiring failures.
compatibleVariants: Discovery 2 TD5 S, Discovery 2 TD5 SE, Discovery 2 TD5 Landmark, Discovery 2 TD5 Commercial
commonFailures: Injector loom connector corrosion causing misfires and erratic fuelling under damp conditions
Cylinder head cracking between valve seats due to thermal cycling (commonly 100k+ miles)
Rocker shaft bearing wear leading to loss of valve timing and top-end clatter
entries.cta: Get quotes for Td5 Land Rover Discovery engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Refined the TDi architecture with improved block rigidity, serving as the final pre-common-rail Discovery diesel unit.
compatibleVariants: Discovery 1 300Tdi ES, Discovery 1 300Tdi SE, Discovery 1 300Tdi County, Discovery 1 300Tdi Commercial
commonFailures: Head gasket failure between adjacent cylinders causing coolant loss and overheating
Preheating relay contacts welding shut, leading to continuous battery drain
Crankshaft pulley rubber damper degradation causing belt drive timing loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for 300Tdi Land Rover Discovery engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5 Litre Diesel
entries.history: A mechanically injected rotary-pump icon, prized for overland simplicity but prone to high-mileage head gasket leaks.
compatibleVariants: Discovery 1 200Tdi Base, Discovery 1 200Tdi LXi, Discovery 1 200Tdi Commercial
commonFailures: Rotary fuel injection pump seal degradation causing internal pressure loss and hard starting
Main bearing cap gasket seepage leading to rapid oil loss on long motorway journeys
Exhaust manifold cracking from thermal stress, often damaging turbocharger housing
entries.cta: Get quotes for 200Tdi Land Rover Discovery engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Introduced the twin-turbo AJD-V8 architecture to replace V8 petrols, offering immense torque but sensitive cooling demands.
compatibleVariants: Discovery 3 TDV8 HSE, Discovery 4 TDV8 SE, Discovery 4 TDV8 HSE
commonFailures: Coolant pipe gasket failure under cylinder banks causing rapid temperature spikes
Turbo oil feed pipe carbon blockage leading to premature turbo bearing seizure
Rocker cover gasket shrinkage resulting in oil contamination of ancillary components
entries.cta: Get quotes for 368DT Land Rover Discovery engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: A supercharged V8 flagship delivering premium performance, though chain tensioner and main bearing wear drive rebuilds.
compatibleVariants: Discovery 4 LR4 V8 HSE, Discovery 4 Si4 HSE Luxury, Discovery 4 V8 Supercharged SVX, Discovery 4 V8 Autobiography
commonFailures: Timing chain guide plastic degradation causing chain jump and catastrophic valve damage (typically 70k-90k miles)
Crankshaft main bearing shell rotation under heavy load, particularly on modified or poorly serviced units
Supercharger intercooler coolant pump failure leading to heat soak and reduced boost pressure
entries.cta: Get quotes for 508PN Land Rover Discovery engine replacement

entries.title: 4.4 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Long-running Rover V8 architecture providing accessible power, though cooling system inadequacies led to frequent head repairs.
compatibleVariants: Discovery 3 V6 HSE, Discovery 3 V8 SE, Discovery 4 Si4 Base, Discovery 4 LR4 V6
Discovery 2 ES, Discovery 2 SE, Discovery 2 GS, Discovery 2 Landmark V8
commonFailures: Cam bearing cap wear causing oil starvation to upper valvetrain components (often 80k+ miles)
Secondary timing chain tensioner rattle due to oil pressure fluctuation at startup
Intake manifold runner actuator failure disrupting air-fuel mixture at highway speeds
Cylinder head gasket failure between coolant and oil passages due to block warping
Distributor O-ring degradation causing sudden misfire and check engine illumination
Water pump impeller corrosion leading to chronic overheating and thermal stress
entries.cta: Get quotes for 14G/17G Land Rover Discovery engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol MHEV
entries.history: Land Rover’s modern mild-hybrid six-cylinder, pairing electric boost with refined highway cruising for the latest Discovery.
compatibleVariants: Discovery 5 P340 SE, Discovery 5 P360 R-Dynamic, Discovery 5 P400 HSE, Discovery 5 P400 Autobiography, Discovery 5 P360 30th Anniversary
commonFailures: 48V belt-integrated starter generator bearing failure causing electrical system limp mode
High-pressure fuel pump timing gear wear under frequent stop-start cycling
Turbo wastegate actuator motor sticking leading to boost pressure instability
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ30P Land Rover Discovery engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol PHEV
entries.history: Discovery 5 plug-in hybrid pairing turbo petrol with electric drive, requiring specialist battery and inverter module handling.
compatibleVariants: Discovery 5 P300e SE, Discovery 5 P300e HSE, Discovery 5 P300e R-Dynamic, Discovery 5 P400e SVAutobiography
commonFailures: High-voltage battery coolant pump failure triggering thermal isolation and reduced EV range
Dual-mass flywheel resonance under hybrid torque transition causing drivetrain vibration
Inverter board capacitor degradation leading to sudden electric motor cutoff and fault codes
entries.cta: Get quotes for P300e Land Rover Discovery engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Land Rover Discovery Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Land Rover Discovery’s off-road capability and heavy towing demands place extraordinary stress on its powertrain, with the 3.0L TDV6/SDV6 diesel and 5.0L V8 petrol engines dominating UK replacement demand due to well-documented weak points. While disciplined servicing extends service life, systemic design flaws in oil delivery and timing drive systems mean terminal failures are surprisingly common on high-mileage examples. Every rebuilt Land Rover Discovery engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components engineered specifically to resolve these original failure points, backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the breakdowns below as a practical decision tool to weigh repair versus replacement costs before committing to a specialist quote.

problems.group: Land Rover Discovery 3.0L V6 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Land Rover Discovery 3.0L V6 Timing Chain Failure - Why TDV6/SDV6 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Land Rover Discovery 4 & 5 SDV6, TDV6, HSE, SE (2009-Present, 306DT & Gen III AJ-V6)
problems.rootCause: The lower timing chain tensioners wear prematurely under sustained high load, causing chain slack that misaligns the oil pump drive gears and leads to inadequate oil pressure at the crankshaft bearings.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Timing Chain & Tensioner Kit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of timing cover, replacement of primary/secondary chains, upgraded tensioners, and recalibration of valve timing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only before rattling or oil pressure faults appear; extends chain service life by 40,000-60,000 miles

repairOptions.tier: Major - Oil Pump & Lower Chain Drive Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal or subframe drop, replacement of failed oil pump drive, lower timing chain set, and pressure testing
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory oil flow; does not reverse existing bearing wear if pressure warning lights have already illuminated

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned 3.0L V6 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete supply & fit of a bench-tested long block, upgraded timing drive components, remanufactured heads, 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; addresses all internal wear points with reinforced bearings and improved tensioner geometry

problems.recommendation: On a Land Rover Discovery 4 or 5 with under 80,000 miles, a preventative timing chain and tensioner refresh is highly cost-effective and significantly delays failure. However, if the warning light has illuminated or you hear metallic rattling at startup, bearing damage has likely occurred and a full rebuilt engine replacement becomes the only reliable, long-term option.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 306DT and Gen III SDV6 engine prices for your Land Rover Discovery

problems.group: Land Rover Discovery 5.0L V8 Timing Chain Guide Failure
problems.h4: Land Rover Discovery 5.0L V8 Timing Chain Guide Failure - Why AJ-V8 Petrol Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Land Rover Discovery 3 & 4 V8 Supercharged & Naturally Aspirated (2005-2016, AJ133 & AJ33)
problems.rootCause: Factory plastic timing chain guides become brittle from repeated heat cycling, shedding fragments into the oil sump that block the pickup strainer and starve the upper valvetrain of lubrication.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Front-end disassembly, installation of reinforced nylon guides, tensioner upgrade, oil pan cleaning, and system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early before guide debris enters the oil pump or cam phasers

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned 5.0L V8 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a complete long block, upgraded steel-reinforced guides, new timing chains, pressure-tested oil gallery, 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory V8 performance with permanently resolved weak points; ideal for vehicles intended for heavy towing or off-road use

problems.recommendation: Given the high labour intensity of stripping the front assembly and the severe damage caused by guide fragmentation, preventative replacement is only viable if scheduled before 90,000 miles. If metal rattling or camshaft timing faults are present, the oil system is already compromised and a full replacement is the only financially sensible route for a Discovery of this age and residual value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AJ133 and AJ33 V8 engine prices for your Land Rover Discovery

problems.group: Land Rover Discovery 2.0L TD4 Turbo & EGR Failure
problems.h4: Land Rover Discovery 2.0L TD4 Turbo & EGR Failure - Why Ingenium Diesel Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Land Rover Discovery 5 S, SE, HSE TD4 / P200D (2017-Present, 204DTD Ingenium)
problems.rootCause: Short urban drive cycles cause excessive soot accumulation in the EGR cooler and turbo oil return line, leading to carbon coking that restricts oil drainage and starves the turbo bearings.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Turbo Oil Feed & EGR Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of turbocharger, carbon cleaning of EGR system, replacement of oil feed/return pipes, and system calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Highly effective if blue smoke or boost hesitation occurs early; prevents turbo shaft seizure

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned 2.0L Ingenium Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete supply & fit of remanufactured block, upgraded turbo oil feed routing, reinforced cylinder liners, 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves underlying oil flow design flaws; best suited for high-mileage vehicles where internal cylinder scoring has begun

problems.recommendation: The 2.0L TD4 responds well to preventative oil feed and EGR servicing when caught at the first signs of boost lag or excessive oil consumption. However, if the turbo shaft has seized or carbon scoring has compromised the cylinder walls, a full rebuild is necessary to avoid recurring failures and ensure the Discovery remains reliable for daily use.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 204DTD Ingenium engine prices for your Land Rover Discovery

problems.group: Land Rover Discovery 2.7L TDV6 Oil Pump Pickup Failure
problems.h4: Land Rover Discovery 2.7L TDV6 Oil Pump Pickup Failure - Why Early Diesel Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Land Rover Discovery 3 TDV6 Passenger & Commercial (2004-2009, DT17 / 27DT)
problems.rootCause: The oil pump pickup strainer becomes clogged by sludge from infrequent oil changes or degraded fuel, causing sudden pressure loss, bearing knock, and eventual crankshaft scoring.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Oil Pump, Strainer & Sump Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Subframe lowering, sump removal, installation of high-flow pump and redesigned pickup strainer, full engine flush
repairOptions.longevity: Restores oil pressure if performed before deep knocking occurs; extends service life by 30,000+ miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned 2.7L V6 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete supply & fit of remanufactured block, new crankshaft bearings, upgraded oil pump system, 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable solution if the engine has experienced prolonged low-pressure operation or shows signs of main bearing failure

problems.recommendation: For early Discovery 3 models with consistent service history but high mileage, upgrading the oil pump and pickup strainer is a proven, cost-effective intervention. If metallic knocking or persistent low-pressure warnings are already present, internal damage is irreversible and a full replacement is the only way to protect the vehicle’s chassis and drivetrain from further strain.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DT17 2.7L TDV6 engine prices for your Land Rover Discovery

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Land Rover Discovery
finalCta.paragraph: When multiple weak points across the TDV6, SDV6, V8, and Ingenium diesel families are left unaddressed, they quickly escalate from minor servicing issues to terminal failure that totals the vehicle’s residual value. EngineMarket removes the guesswork by connecting you directly with vetted UK specialists who understand exactly how Discovery engines fail and how to fix them permanently. Every quote includes a fully rebuilt or reconditioned unit featuring upgraded components that eliminate the original design flaws, complete with bench testing and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices today and secure a specialist-backed replacement that keeps your Discovery road-ready without the main dealer markup.

engineSizes.h2: Land Rover Discovery Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every official UK-fitted displacement for the Land Rover Discovery is listed below, complete with original engine codes, production years across generations, and key failure patterns to watch for during replacement.

groups.title: Land Rover Discovery Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Land Rover Discovery 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced on the fifth-generation Discovery 5 (L462), the 2.0L Ingenium inline-four serves as the entry-level diesel, balancing modern emissions compliance with improved fuel economy for daily driving. Land Rover integrated lightweight aluminium architecture and a variable-geometry turbocharger to meet stringent Euro 6 standards without sacrificing low-end torque. This compact unit remains a popular choice for budget-conscious replacements due to its widespread availability and straightforward service intervals.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear
DPF-related oil dilution causing bearing stress on early build years
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover Discovery 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Land Rover Discovery 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: Deployed exclusively during the Discovery 4 facelift cycle, the 2.2L SD4 replaced the older V6 diesel as the mid-range efficiency option. Built on the Ford/PSA Duratorq architecture but refined by Land Rover for heavy SUV duty, it features a twin-scroll turbo and revised engine management to deliver smooth, linear power delivery. Its compact inline-four layout simplifies front-end servicing compared to the wider V-configuration predecessors.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo actuator electronic failures
EGR cooler matrix cracking
carbon buildup on intake valves
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover Discovery 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Land Rover Discovery 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of the first two Discovery generations, the 2.5L direct-injection diesel evolved through the 200Tdi, 300Tdi, and Td5 iterations, cementing Land Rover’s off-road legacy. Early cast-iron blocks were prized for over-engineered durability and straightforward mechanical servicing, while the Td5 introduced electronic engine management and common-rail injection for refined highway cruising. Today, it remains a highly sought-after classic replacement engine due to its legendary torque curve and parts availability.
commonFailurePoints: Cylinder head cracking on early Tdi blocks
oil pan gasket seepage
Td5 injector wiring harness moisture corrosion
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover Discovery 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Land Rover Discovery 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: Powering the launch generation of the Discovery 3, the 2.7L TDV6 represents Land Rover’s shift toward modern, refined six-cylinder diesels developed alongside PSA. Featuring a 60-degree V-configuration with chain-driven overhead cams and advanced common-rail injection, it was engineered to deliver V8-level torque with diesel efficiency for a heavier, coil-sprung chassis. Despite its early adoption of sophisticated emissions tech, it established the platform’s long-distance touring credentials.
commonFailurePoints: Camshaft bearing wear due to oil pressure fluctuations
crankshaft pulley separation
turbo oil feed pipe degradation
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover Discovery 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Land Rover Discovery 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Serving as the definitive diesel across the Discovery 4 and 5 generations, the 3.0L V6 and later inline-six Ingenium units form the core of the premium SUV’s powertrain lineup. Initially launched as a refined 306DT V6 with twin turbos, Land Rover later transitioned to a longitudinally mounted 3.0L mild-hybrid I6 to improve weight distribution, NVH levels, and torque response. Its advanced thermal management and high-pressure injection systems make it the most technologically sophisticated oil-burning option in the range.
commonFailurePoints: V6 timing chain guide wear on pre-2014 blocks
water pump bearing failure
early Ingenium I6 crankshaft main bearing spin on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Get quotes for Land Rover Discovery 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Land Rover Discovery Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Land Rover Discovery 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced as a premium petrol alternative to diesel in the Discovery 4 facelift and carried into the Discovery 5, the 3.0L supercharged V6 delivers brisk acceleration and refined NVH characteristics. Developed in partnership with Jaguar, the engine utilises a twin-rotor Eaton supercharger and direct injection to provide seamless power delivery suited to luxury touring. Its compact packaging allows for better front-axle weight distribution compared to the larger V8 options.
commonFailurePoints: Supercharger coupler wear causing whining noises
plastic thermostat housing cracking under thermal stress
timing chain tensioner rattle on cold starts
items.cta: Compare Land Rover Discovery 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Land Rover Discovery 4.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: Exclusively fitted to the early Discovery 3 launch models, the 4.4L naturally aspirated V8 provides a traditional, torque-rich driving experience aligned with the vehicle’s premium positioning. Derived from Jaguar’s AJ37 architecture but heavily revised by Land Rover for low-end grunt, it features dual overhead cams per bank and variable valve timing for smooth highway refinement. Though phased out in favour of supercharged units, it remains a staple for owners seeking reliability and classic V8 character.
commonFailurePoints: Valley pan gasket oil seepage
timing chain guide plastic degradation
ignition coil pack failure causing cylinder misfires
items.cta: Compare Land Rover Discovery 4.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Land Rover Discovery 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Representing the performance flagship of the Discovery 4 and early Discovery 5 range, the 5.0L supercharged V8 delivers flagship-level acceleration and towing capability. Built entirely in aluminium by Jaguar Land Rover’s in-house foundry, it utilises a Gen III block architecture, direct injection, and an intercooled twin-screw supercharger to achieve high specific output while maintaining structural rigidity. This unit is engineered for longevity under heavy loads but requires strict adherence to factory oil specifications and warm-up protocols.
commonFailurePoints: Cam phaser bolt wear leading to startup rattle
supercharger Eaton coupler failure
crankshaft main bearing spin due to extended oil service intervals
items.cta: Compare Land Rover Discovery 5.0L petrol engine prices

P360 - 3.0L inline-six petrol supercharged with electric torque assist

engineYears.h2: Land Rover Discovery Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major Land Rover Discovery engine changes by year, helping owners identify their engine and compare UK rebuilt replacement prices from verified specialists.

years.description: The L319 platform launched with the 2.7L TDV6 (276DT) - a complex twin-turbo diesel that defined Discovery 3 ownership but introduced oil pump and timing chain vulnerabilities.
keyChanges: All-new L319 monocoque chassis replaced traditional ladder frame
2.7L Ford/PSA-derived TDV6 (276DT) introduced as sole diesel option
Terrain Response system debuted with engine mapping integration
Electronic air suspension (ECAS) became standard on higher trims
knownFor: 276DT oil pump casing failure risk - preventative upgrade recommended
Complex electronics and air suspension increase repair costs
Strong off-road capability but higher running costs than Discovery 2
Timing chain tensioners require monitoring beyond 100k miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2009 Land Rover Discovery 3 replacement engines
years.ticker: Land Rover Discovery 3 2.7 TDV6 engine replacement • Discovery 3 oil pump upgrade cost • 276DT timing chain replacement UK
enquiries: Land Rover Discovery 3 2.7 TDV6 engine replacement

years.description: Discovery 4 retained the L319 platform but replaced the 2.7L with the more powerful 3.0L SDV6 (306DT), addressing some reliability concerns while introducing new failure patterns.
keyChanges: 3.0L SDV6 (306DT) replaced 2.7L TDV6 across the range
Revised front-end styling and interior refinement
Improved ZF 6HP automatic transmission calibration
Enhanced Terrain Response 2 with additional off-road modes
knownFor: 306DT turbo actuator faults (P00Q6 code) common on high-mileage examples
EGR cooler failures leading to coolant loss and overheating
More robust than 276DT but still requires timing chain monitoring
Strong towing capability makes it popular with commercial users
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2014 Land Rover Discovery 4 replacement engines
years.ticker: Land Rover Discovery 4 3.0 SDV6 engine replacement • 306DT turbo actuator fault fix • Discovery 4 EGR cooler replacement cost
enquiries: Land Rover Discovery 4 3.0 SDV6 engine replacement

years.description: The final Discovery 4 model years received the Gen2 306DT engine with revised oil pump casing and updated timing components, reducing catastrophic failure risk.
keyChanges: Gen2 306DT engine with reinforced oil pump housing introduced
Updated timing chain tensioners and guides for improved longevity
Minor interior trim updates and infotainment enhancements
Final production run before L462 Discovery 5 launch
popularModels: Land Rover Discovery 4 SDV6 HSE Luxury
knownFor: Gen2 306DT significantly reduces oil pump failure risk vs pre-2015 engines
Best-value Discovery 4 for used buyers seeking reliability improvements
Still susceptible to turbo and EGR issues on high-mileage examples
Strong residual values due to proven capability and updated reliability
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2016 Land Rover Discovery 4 Gen2 replacement engines
years.ticker: Land Rover Discovery 4 Gen2 306DT engine • Discovery 4 2015 oil pump upgrade • 306DT timing chain Gen2 replacement

years.description: The all-new L462 Discovery 5 switched to unibody construction and introduced Jaguar Land Rover's Ingenium 2.0L and 3.0L engines, bringing new technology and new failure patterns.
keyChanges: New L462 aluminium-intensive unibody platform replaced L319
Ingenium AJ200D 2.0L diesel and AJ200P 2.0L petrol engines introduced
3.0L 306DT carried over but with updated ECU calibration
8-speed ZF automatic transmission became standard across the range
knownFor: AJ200D Ingenium timing chain wear due to oil dilution - major failure pattern
Unibody construction improves on-road refinement but reduces extreme off-road durability
Complex electronics and air suspension increase diagnostic complexity
Strong demand for 306DT engines as owners avoid Ingenium reliability concerns
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2019 Land Rover Discovery 5 replacement engines
years.ticker: Land Rover Discovery 5 AJ200D timing chain replacement • Discovery 5 2.0 Ingenium engine failure • 306DT vs AJ200D reliability comparison
enquiries: Land Rover Discovery 5 AJ200D timing chain replacement

years.description: The 2020 facelift introduced mild-hybrid technology to the D300 and D350 variants, using updated 306DT-based engines with 48V electrical systems for improved efficiency.
keyChanges: Mild-hybrid 48V system added to D300/D350 diesel variants
Updated P360 3.0L inline-6 petrol with mild-hybrid assistance
Revised exterior styling and upgraded Pivi Pro infotainment
Enhanced driver assistance systems and OTA update capability
mainEngines: AJ200D - 2.0L diesel (D250, continuing in lower trims)
popularModels: Land Rover Discovery 5 D350 HSE Luxury
knownFor: Mild-hybrid system adds complexity but improves fuel economy and low-end torque
AJ300D retains 306DT core architecture - timing chain monitoring still advised
P360 petrol variant offers strong performance but higher replacement costs
Latest models benefit from updated ECU software addressing early Ingenium issues

years.ticker: Land Rover Discovery D300 mild hybrid engine • Discovery 5 P360 petrol replacement cost • AJ300D timing chain warranty claim

faq.h2: Land Rover Discovery Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Land Rover Discovery engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: A Land Rover Discovery engine replacement costs from £2,850 for a reconditioned unit to £9,100 for high-performance petrol variants, supply-only. Supply & fit packages typically add £1,200-£2,500 depending on workshop location. Prices vary by engine code, with 306DT diesel the most common and competitively priced option across Discovery 4 and 5 models.
keyPoints: Rebuilt 2.7 TDV6 engines start around £3,200 supply-only UK-wide
3.0 SDV6 306DT replacements range £3,450-£5,600 depending on mileage
Ingenium 2.0L AJ200D units cost £3,600-£5,100 due to timing chain complexity
V8 petrol 508PN engines command £5,800-£7,900 for specialist rebuilds
All quotes include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard
items.cta: Get your free Land Rover Discovery engine replacement quote in 60 seconds

items.question: What is the most common Land Rover Discovery engine problem UK owners report?
items.answer: Timing chain failure on the 2.0L Ingenium diesel (AJ200D) and oil pump casing failure on 2.7/3.0 TDV6 units (276DT/306DT) dominate Land Rover Discovery engine fault reports. Both issues cause catastrophic damage if warning signs like rattling or low oil pressure are ignored, making early diagnosis critical for cost-effective replacement decisions.
keyPoints: AJ200D timing chains wear prematurely due to oil dilution from DPF regeneration cycles
276DT/306DT oil pumps suffer casing cracks leading to sudden oil pressure loss
Turbocharger actuator faults (P00Q6 code) frequently affect twin-turbo 306DT engines
EGR cooler leaks cause coolant loss and overheating on high-mileage TDV6 units
Early symptom recognition can prevent total engine seizure and reduce replacement costs
items.cta: Compare Land Rover Discovery engine replacement prices for your specific engine code

items.question: How do I find my Land Rover Discovery engine code quickly?
items.answer: Your Land Rover Discovery engine code appears on the V5C registration document under "engine number", on the engine bay identification plate, or by entering your reg on our free lookup tool. The most common codes are 276DT (2.7 TDV6), 306DT (3.0 SDV6), and AJ200D (2.0 Ingenium diesel).
keyPoints: Check V5C section D.2 for the factory engine code reference
Engine bay plaque near the firewall lists code and serial number
Discovery 3 (2004-2009) typically uses 276DT 2.7L V6 diesel
Discovery 4/5 (2010-present) predominantly feature 306DT 3.0L units
AJ200D 2.0L Ingenium appears in late-model Discovery 5 and Sport
items.cta: Enter your reg to instantly identify your Land Rover Discovery engine code

items.question: Is it better to rebuild or replace a Land Rover Discovery engine?
items.answer: For Land Rover Discovery engines suffering catastrophic failure like seized crankshafts or snapped timing chains, a professionally rebuilt replacement engine offers superior value and warranty protection versus on-site rebuilds. Reconditioned units from our UK specialists include bench-testing, updated failure-prone components, and a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines replace all wear items: bearings, seals, timing components, oil pump
Supply-only prices start £2,850; supply & fit adds £1,200-£2,500 workshop labour
Rebuilds on-vehicle risk missing hidden damage like crank scoring or head cracks
Warranty coverage on replacement engines protects against premature repeat failure
Core return policy refunds £300-£800 if original block is undamaged
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Land Rover Discovery engine prices from verified UK suppliers

items.question: What warranty comes with a Land Rover Discovery replacement engine?
items.answer: Every Land Rover Discovery replacement engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering all major internal components. This includes crankshaft, pistons, cylinder heads, turbochargers, and high-pressure fuel systems. Warranty terms are transferable between owners and valid at any UK garage for peace of mind.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on all rebuilt and reconditioned units
Coverage includes crankshaft, pistons, cylinder heads, turbos, and fuel injection systems
Warranty is transferable if you sell your Land Rover Discovery within the term
Claims processed directly with the supplying specialist, no dealer involvement required
Supply & fit packages include labour warranty for installation workmanship
items.cta: Find a Land Rover Discovery replacement engine with full 12-month warranty coverage

items.question: Do you offer Land Rover Discovery engine supply and fit near me?
items.answer: Yes, our network of Land Rover Discovery engine specialists covers all UK mainland postcodes for supply and fit engine replacement. Once you compare quotes, your chosen workshop arranges collection or delivery of your vehicle, completes the engine swap in 2-3 days, and handles core return logistics for your old unit.
keyPoints: Over 120 verified Land Rover specialist garages across England, Scotland, Wales
Fixed-price fitting quotes include all labour, fluids, and diagnostic recalibration
Most workshops offer courtesy cars or collection/delivery services for convenience
Engine replacement typically completed in 2-3 working days from arrival
All fitting centres follow JLR-spec torque settings and calibration procedures
items.cta: Book your Land Rover Discovery engine supply and fit appointment with a local specialist

items.question: How do I know if my Land Rover Discovery needs a new engine?
items.answer: If your Land Rover Discovery shows persistent knocking from the bottom end, zero oil pressure on the dash, or white smoke with coolant loss, these indicate catastrophic internal failure requiring engine replacement. Less severe issues like turbo faults or EGR problems may be repairable without full Land Rover Discovery engine replacement.
keyPoints: Deep knocking or rattling from the crankshaft area signals bearing failure
Zero oil pressure warning with no visible leaks suggests pump or pickup failure
White exhaust smoke with sweet smell indicates coolant entering combustion chambers
Seized engine that won't turn over confirms internal mechanical destruction
Diagnostic codes P0016, P0087, or P00Q6 often precede major Land Rover Discovery engine failure
items.cta: Get a rapid assessment for your Land Rover Discovery engine symptoms from UK specialists

items.question: What is the cheapest way to replace a Land Rover Discovery engine?
items.answer: The cheapest Land Rover Discovery engine replacement option is a grade-A used engine from a verified breaker, starting around £2,350 supply-only. However, reconditioned units from £2,850 offer better long-term value with warranty protection, updated failure-prone components, and bench-testing that used engines cannot guarantee for peace of mind.
keyPoints: Used engines from breakers start £2,350 but carry unknown mileage and history
Reconditioned engines from £2,850 include new bearings, seals, and timing components
Core return refunds of £300-£800 reduce net cost if original block is sound
Supply-only option saves £1,200-£2,500 if you have a trusted local fitter
Avoid non-UK suppliers; import duties and shipping often erase apparent savings
items.cta: Compare the cheapest Land Rover Discovery engine replacement quotes from UK verified suppliers

items.question: Which Land Rover Discovery engine is most reliable and which should I avoid?
items.answer: The 3.0L SDV6 (306DT) in Discovery 4 and early Discovery 5 models offers the best balance of reliability and performance when oil pump upgrades are fitted. Avoid the 2.0L Ingenium diesel (AJ200D) for high-mileage use due to timing chain vulnerability, and be cautious with pre-2015 306DT units lacking Gen2 oil pump revisions.
keyPoints: 306DT Gen2 (2015+) features revised oil pump casing preventing catastrophic failure
AJ200D Ingenium diesel requires strict oil changes every 10k miles to protect timing chain
276DT 2.7L TDV6 is robust but oil pump upgrades are essential preventative maintenance
Petrol V8 units (508PN) are rare but mechanically simple with fewer failure points
Regular servicing with JLR-spec oil is critical for all Land Rover Discovery engine longevity
items.cta: Find a reliable Land Rover Discovery replacement engine with updated failure-prone components

items.question: How long should a Land Rover Discovery engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: With strict adherence to JLR service intervals and use of approved oils, Land Rover Discovery diesel engines (276DT, 306DT) commonly reach 150,000-200,000 miles before major overhaul. The 2.0L Ingenium AJ200D requires more vigilant maintenance; timing chain issues can emerge before 100,000 miles if oil changes are delayed or DPF regeneration cycles are excessive.
keyPoints: 306DT engines with Gen2 oil pump revisions regularly exceed 180,000 miles trouble-free
AJ200D Ingenium units need oil changes every 10k miles, not the official 18k
Turbocharger lifespan averages 120,000 miles; premature failure often signals underlying issues
Using non-spec oil accelerates timing chain wear on all Land Rover Discovery diesel engines
Full service history significantly increases resale value and reduces replacement engine costs
items.cta: Check Land Rover Discovery engine longevity data and replacement options for your model year

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Land Rover Discovery?
items.answer: Fitting a different engine code in your Land Rover Discovery is technically possible but risks MOT failure, insurance invalidation, and emissions non-compliance. Stick to the original factory engine code (276DT, 306DT, AJ200D, or 508PN) for your model year to ensure legal road use and retain warranty coverage on your Land Rover Discovery engine replacement.
keyPoints: Engine swaps must match original emissions standard for MOT compliance
ECU recalibration is required if changing between diesel and petrol variants
Insurance providers may void coverage for unreported engine code modifications
Specialist Land Rover garages can advise on compatible Land Rover Discovery engine codes
Using the correct code preserves Terrain Response and air suspension functionality
items.cta: Verify your Land Rover Discovery engine code compatibility before ordering a replacement

ui.warningTitle: Check fitment and engine condition before ordering

points.title: Trusted Land Rover Discovery Engine Suppliers

trustCta.finalText: Find the best Land Rover Discovery engine replacement UK drivers rely on. Compare prices from trusted specialists today and get back on the road with confidence.
